messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Last 3 Seats Available: Functional Trait Space Analyses in R
messages.subject_clean: Last 3 Seats Available: Functional Trait Space Analyses in R
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We have just 3 seats remaining for our online course, Functional Trait Space Analyses in R, taking place from September 23-25, 2024.
 
 
Course Highlights:
Dates: 23-25 September 2024
Time: 14:00 - 18:00 (Berlin time)
Format: Online, with live lectures and hands-on sessions in R
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/functional-trait-space-analyses-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/functional-trait-space-analyses-in-r/ ) 
This course offers PhD students and postdocs a comprehensive introduction to functional trait space analyses, a crucial tool in trait-based ecology. You?ll learn to build, interpret, and link functional trait spaces to ecological dimensions using R, with practical applications of key packages like smatr, TPD, and funspace.
 
 
Learning Outcomes:
Master the theory and methods behind trait space analyses
Develop skills in plotting and exporting analysis results
Gain practical experience with R packages used in trait-based ecology
Don?t miss this opportunity to enhance your research skills. Secure your spot now!
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Zero-inflated GAMs for the analysis of spatial and spatial-temporal correlated data using R-INLA
messages.subject_clean: Zero-inflated GAMs for the analysis of spatial and spatial-temporal correlated data using R-INLA
messages.body_plain:
Join our course on advanced statistics! You can attend in person or 
online via Zoom.

Zero-inflated GAMs for the analysis of spatial and spatial-temporal 
correlated data using R-INLA


*Course details*

  * When: September 23 - 27, 2024
  * Where: Hotel de Nieuwe Wereld, Wageningen, The Netherlands
  * Time: 9 AM to 4 PM CET

*
*

*What you will learn:
*

  * Understanding the basics: Learn the fundamentals of Bayesian
    statistics and how to use R-INLA for standard GLMs.
  * Handling Sparse Data: Discover techniques to manage data sets with a
    high number of zeros in both counts and continuous data, using R-INLA.
  * Incorporating non-linear relationships: Explore how to incorporate
    non-linear covariate effects using generalized additive models
    (GAMs) in R-INLA.
  * Accounting for spatial and temporal Effects: Learn how to integrate
    the influences of space and time into your models.
  * Synthesizing the concepts: Bring all these elements together to
    build comprehensive statistical models.


Course flyer: 
https://www.highstat.com/Courses/Flyers/Flyer2024_09_ZIGAMSpatTemp.pdf

Registration: www.highstat.com


We hope to see you there and help you grow your skills in statistical 
modelling skills!

Kind regards,

Alain Zuur

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Multivariate Analysis Of Ecological Communities Using R With The VEGAN package (VGNR06) - date change
messages.subject_clean: Multivariate Analysis Of Ecological Communities Using R With The VEGAN package (VGNR06) - date change
messages.body_plain:
ONLINE COURSE ? Multivariate Analysis Of Ecological Communities Using R
With The VEGAN package (VGNR06)

https://www.prstats.org/course/multivariate-analysis-of-ecological-communities-using-r-with-the-vegan-package-vgnr06/

*NEW DATES!!! *23-27th September 2024

Please feel free to share!

This 5-day course will cover R concepts, methods, and tools that can be
used to analyze community ecology data. The course will review data
processing techniques relevant to multivariate data sets. We will cover
diversity indices, distance measures and distance-based multivariate
methods, clustering, classification and ordination techniques using the R
package VEGAN. We will use real-world empirical data sets to motivate
analyses, such as describing patterns along gradients of environ-mental or
anthropogenic disturbances, quantifying the effects of continuous and
discrete predictors. We will emphasise visualisation and reproducible
workflows as well as good programming practices. The modules will consist
of introductory lectures, guided computer coding, and participant
exercises. The course is intended for intermediate users of R who are
interested in community ecology, particularly in the areas of terrestrial
and wetland ecology, microbial ecology, and natural resource management.
You are strongly encouraged to use your own data sets (they should be clean
and already structured, see the document: ?recommendation if you
participate with your data?.

Please email oliverhooker at prstatistics.com with any questions

Upcoming courses
ONLINE COURSE ? Reproducible and collaborative data analysis with R
(RACR03) This course will be delivered live
<https://www.prstats.org/course/reproducible-and-collaborative-data-analysis-with-r-racr03/>

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] ONLINE COURSE – Time Series Analysis and Forecasting using R and Rstudio (TSAF01)
messages.subject_clean: ONLINE COURSE – Time Series Analysis and Forecasting using R and Rstudio (TSAF01)
messages.body_plain:
ONLINE COURSE ? Time Series Analysis and Forecasting using R and Rstudio
(TSAF01)

We still have a few places available for our upcoming course on time series
and forecasting in a couple of weeks time.

https://www.prstats.org/course/time-series-analysis-and-forecasting-using-r-and-rstudio-tsaf01/

Instructor - Dr. Rafael De Andrade Moral

17th - 26th October

Please feel free to share!

In this six-day course (Approx. 35 hours), we provide a comprehensive
practical and theoretical introduction to time series analysis and
forecasting methods using R. Forecasting tools are useful in many areas,
such as finance, meteorology, ecology, public policy, and health. We start
by introducing the concepts of time series and stationarity, which will
help us when studying ARIMA-type models. We will also cover autocorrelation
functions and series decomposition methods. Then, we will introduce
benchmark forecasting methods, namely the na?ve (or random walk) method,
mean, drift, and seasonal na?ve methods. After that, we will present
different exponential smoothing methods (simple, Holt?s linear method, and
Holt-Winters seasonal method). We will then cover autoregressive integrated
moving-average (or ARIMA) models, with and without seasonality. We will
also cover Generalized Additive Models (GAMs) and how they can be used to
incorporate seasonality effects in the analysis of time series data.
Finally, we will cover Bayesian implementations of time series models and
introduce extended models, such as ARCH, GARCH and stochastic volatility
models, as well as Brownian motion and Ornstein-Uhlenbeck processes.

Please email oliverhooker at prstatistics.com with any questions.

-- 
Best wishes,

Oliver

Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Bioacoustics Data Analysis using R and Studio (BIAC04)
messages.subject_clean: Bioacoustics Data Analysis using R and Studio (BIAC04)
messages.body_plain:
Bioacoustics Data Analysis using R and Studio (BIAC04)

https://www.prstats.org/course/bioacoustics-data-analysis-biac04/https://www.prstats.org/course/bioacoustics-data-analysis-biac04/
ABOUT THIS COURSE

The study of animal acoustic signals is a central tool for many fields in
behavior, ecology, evolution and biodiversity monitoring. The accessibility
of recording equipment and growing availability of open-access acoustic
libraries provide an unprecedented opportunity to study animal acoustic
signals at large temporal, geographic and taxonomic scales. However, the
diversity of analytical methods and the multidimensionality of these
signals posts significant challenges to conduct analyses that can quantify
biologically meaningful variation. The recent development of acoustic
analysis tools in the R programming environment provides a powerful means
for overcoming these challenges, facilitating the gathering and
organization of large acoustic data sets and the use of more elaborated
analyses that better fit the studied acoustic signals and associated
biological questions. The course will introduce students on the basic
concepts in animal acoustic signal research as well as hands-on experience
on analytical tools in R.

By the end of the course, participants should:

   - Understand the basic concepts of bioacoustics and how animal acoustic
   signals are analyzed
   - Gain proficiency in handling and manipulating acoustic data in R,
   including working with ?wave? objects and other audio formats
   - Develop skills in building and interpreting spectrograms using Fourier
   transform techniques and the seewave package in R
   - Import Raven Pro annotations into R and refine these annotations with
   warbleR functions
   - Understand how to quantify the structure of acoustic signals through
   various approaches
   - Gain experience in quality control of recordings and annotations,
   ensuring data integrity and accuracy
   - Compare different methods for quantifying acoustic signal structure
   and understand the implications of each approach

Please email oliverhooker at prstatistics.com any questions.

-- 
Best wishes,

Oliver

Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Join Our Online Course: Reproducibility Data Analysis with R (28-31 October 2024)
messages.subject_clean: Join Our Online Course: Reproducibility Data Analysis with R (28-31 October 2024)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are excited to announce our upcoming online course: Reproducibility Data Analysis with R, taking place from 28-31 October. This course is designed to help researchers, data scientists, and R users enhance the reproducibility and collaboration of their projects.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/r-reproducibility/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/r-reproducibility/ ) 
 
Course Overview:
Have you ever struggled with sharing R code that doesn't work on another machine or even fails when you try to rerun it yourself after some time? This course will teach you how to organize your projects to avoid these problems by leveraging tools in the R ecosystem, such as RMarkdown/Quarto, renv, Git, and Docker.
 
 
What You?ll Learn:
Create reproducible R projects
Manage environments and dependencies with renv
Collaborate using Git & GitHub
Share and publish data and containers using Docker
Target Audience:
This course is ideal for researchers, data scientists, and anyone who uses R to generate documents and wants to collaborate with others or future-proof their work. Basic knowledge of R is recommended, but no advanced skills are required.
 
Course Format:
Dates: 28-31 October 2024
Time: 9 AM - 12 PM (Berlin time) each day
Format: Online live lectures, coding, and exercises
Asynchronous support via Slack for homework
Don't miss this opportunity to improve your R skills and make your work more reproducible and collaborative!
 
Best regards,
 
Carlo
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online course: Seascape Genomics in R (October 28 - November 1)
messages.subject_clean: Online course: Seascape Genomics in R (October 28 - November 1)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are pleased to inform you about our upcoming online course, Seascape Genomics in R, taking place from October 28 - November 1. This course offers a unique opportunity to explore how the underwater environment influences the genomic diversity of marine organisms through a combination of environmental data and genomic analyses.
 
 
Course Highlights:
Learn to extract and analyse environmental data (e.g., sea temperature, water movements)
Explore methods to assess genetic structures and connectivity in marine populations
Discover techniques for studying local adaptation and interpreting results for conservation
Target Audience:

This course is ideal for biologists, ecologists, geneticists, veterinarians, and conservationists interested in applying seascape genomics in their research. Basic knowledge of evolutionary biology, population genetics, R, and statistics is recommended.
 
 
Best regards,
 
Carlo
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] FINAL CALL – Hidden Markov Models for Movement, Acceleration and other Ecological Data – an introduction using moveHMM and momentuHMM in R (HMMM01)
messages.subject_clean: FINAL CALL – Hidden Markov Models for Movement, Acceleration and other Ecological Data – an introduction using moveHMM and momentuHMM in R (HMMM01)
messages.body_plain:
ONLINE COURSE ? Hidden Markov Models for Movement, Acceleration and other
Ecological Data ? an introduction using moveHMM and momentuHMM in R (HMMM01)

https://www.prstats.org/course/hidden-markov-models-for-movement-acceleration-and-other-ecological-data-hmmm01/

18th - 21st September 2024

Please feel free to share.

Instructor - Dr. Roland Langrock

ABOUT THIS COURSE; Hidden Markov models (HMMs) are flexible statistical
models for time series observations driven byunderlying states. Over the
last decade, HMMs have become increasingly popular within the ecological
community as they allow to uncover behavioural state dynamics from noisy
sensor data. For example, a typical HMM-based analysis of say GPS locations
or acceleration measurements could involve the investigation of internal
(e.g. sex, size, age) and external (e.g. temperature, habitat) drivers of
behavioural state occupancy.

This workshop will introduce the HMM framework, comprising a mix of
theoretical lectures and hands-on practical components using R. In the
theoretical sessions, the following topics will be covered:
? motivation & overview
? basic HMM formulation
? fitting an HMM to data
? model selection & model checking
? state decoding
? incorporating covariates, seasonality and random effects
? other model extensions

These techniques will be illustrated primarily using movement and
acceleration data, but are applicable also to other ecological time series
data (e.g. capture-recapture). In the practical sessions, we will focus on
HMM analyses using the R packages moveHMM and momentuHMM, but will also
showcase the use of hmmTMB. Basic knowledge of the free software R is
helpful, but not required.

A basic understanding of statistics and probability calculus, as it would
be taught in any introductory statistics class, is required. By the end of
the course, participants will have a good understanding of what HMMs are
and what they can be used for. Participants will also be prepared to tailor
a suitable HMM to their data and to implement the corresponding analysis in
R.

Please email oliverhooker at prstatistics.com with any questions

-- 
Best wishes,

Oliver

Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Join Our Metabolomics with R/Bioconductor Online Course
messages.subject_clean: Join Our Metabolomics with R/Bioconductor Online Course
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
there are 4 seats available for our Metabolomics with R/Bioconductor course, which will take place online from October 7-10.
 
This course covers essential aspects of metabolomics from a data analysis perspective, including study design, data pre-processing, and statistical analysis. It combines lectures, hands-on practicals, and group discussions.
 
The objective of the course is to make participants familiar with the analysis analysis of metabolomic data (targeted and untargeted) in R. The course will also constitute an excellent primer to the application of univariate and multivariate statistics to complex datasets.



Prerequisites: Familiarity with R is assumed, though a day will be devoted to R basics.
 
For more information, please visit: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course55/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course55/ )
 
For the full list of our courses and workshops, please visit: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course55/ )
 
 
Best regards,
 
Carlo
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] ONLINE COURSE – Introduction to R using Tidyverse (TIDY01)
messages.subject_clean: ONLINE COURSE – Introduction to R using Tidyverse (TIDY01)
messages.body_plain:
ONLINE COURSE ? Introduction to R using Tidyverse (TIDY01)

https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-to-r-using-tidyverse-tidy01/

24th - 26th September

Please feel free to share!

R is a popular open-source programming language used by data scientists,
statisticians,
social & health researchers, and many more across a wide range of
disciplines. R is
particularly appealing to researchers as it is free to access, designed to
encourage
collaborative and reproducible data analysis, and is constantly evolving to
include the latest research developments.

This course is designed to equip PhD students, academics, and professional
researchers
across various disciplines with the essential skills to leverage the power
of R and Tidyverse for their academic research. The workshop begins with a
gentle introduction to the user-friendly RStudio interface and the basics
of the R coding language, or syntax. This makes it ideal for anyone with
little or no prior coding experience, or those looking for a refresher of
the basics.

Attendees will learn how to manipulate, transform, and clean data
efficiently, and how to
create compelling visualisations to communicate their findings effectively.
Throughout the
course, we will discuss best practices for reproducible coding. The
workshop is designed to be highly interactive with a focus on practical
applications, ensuring that you can
immediately apply what you learn to your research.

By the end of the course, participants will:
? Understand the RStudio interface and be confident using R syntax
? Be able to load, tidy, and manipulate their data using Tidyverse packages
? Generate clear summary tables that can be customised and exported
? Create compelling, informative data visualisations using ggplot2, and
understand
what makes a good visualisation
? Understand what is meant by reproducible coding, and ensure their scripts
are clear,
tidy and reproducible.
Please email oliverhooker at prstatistics.com with any questions

All our live course and recorded courses can be found using the links below
https://www.prstats.org/live-courses/
https://www.prstats.org/recorded-courses/

-- 
Best wishes,

Oliver

Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] ONLINE COURSES - ML for Computational Biology and DNA Methylation
messages.subject_clean: ONLINE COURSES - ML for Computational Biology and DNA Methylation
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Last chance to register for the upcoming courses on Machine Learning for
Computational Biology for R and Python, running Sept 16, and Analysis of
DNA Methylation using R and Bash, running Sept 18 - 20.

https://instats.org/seminar/machine-learning-for-computational-biolo2

https://instats.org/seminar/analysis-of-dna-methylation2

These courses provide the foundational knowledge needed to begin analyzing
complex datasets across biostatistics, genetics, bioinformatics, ecology,
and more. For the veterinary epidemiology community, the Machine Learning
for Computational Biology course will provide unique insights into how to
understand and analyze advanced computational methods to analyze large
datasets, improving disease modeling and prediction in animal populations.
The Analysis of DNA Methylation course offers valuable tools for
understanding epigenetic factors in animal health, potentially enhancing
research in areas like disease susceptibility and treatment response.

Sign up today to secure your spots in these workshops and we hope to see
you there!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
instats.org

We are pleased to announce the Metabolomics with R/Bioconductor online course, taking place from 7-10 October.

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Bioacoustics Data Analysis using R and Studio (BIAC04)
messages.subject_clean: Bioacoustics Data Analysis using R and Studio (BIAC04)
messages.body_plain:
Bioacoustics Data Analysis using R and Studio (BIAC04)

https://www.prstats.org/course/bioacoustics-data-analysis-biac04/https://www.prstats.org/course/bioacoustics-data-analysis-biac04/
ABOUT THIS COURSE

The study of animal acoustic signals is a central tool for many fields in
behavior, ecology, evolution and biodiversity monitoring. The accessibility
of recording equipment and growing availability of open-access acoustic
libraries provide an unprecedented opportunity to study animal acoustic
signals at large temporal, geographic and taxonomic scales. However, the
diversity of analytical methods and the multidimensionality of these
signals posts significant challenges to conduct analyses that can quantify
biologically meaningful variation. The recent development of acoustic
analysis tools in the R programming environment provides a powerful means
for overcoming these challenges, facilitating the gathering and
organization of large acoustic data sets and the use of more elaborated
analyses that better fit the studied acoustic signals and associated
biological questions. The course will introduce students on the basic
concepts in animal acoustic signal research as well as hands-on experience
on analytical tools in R.

By the end of the course, participants should:

   - Understand the basic concepts of bioacoustics and how animal acoustic
   signals are analyzed
   - Gain proficiency in handling and manipulating acoustic data in R,
   including working with ?wave? objects and other audio formats
   - Develop skills in building and interpreting spectrograms using Fourier
   transform techniques and the seewave package in R
   - Import Raven Pro annotations into R and refine these annotations with
   warbleR functions
   - Understand how to quantify the structure of acoustic signals through
   various approaches
   - Gain experience in quality control of recordings and annotations,
   ensuring data integrity and accuracy
   - Compare different methods for quantifying acoustic signal structure
   and understand the implications of each approach

Please email oliverhooker at prstatistics.com any questions.

-- 
Best wishes,

Oliver

Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] IN PERSON COURSE – Hierarchical modelling in ecology (HMIE01) (Université de Sherbrooke, Canada)
messages.subject_clean: IN PERSON COURSE – Hierarchical modelling in ecology (HMIE01) (Université de Sherbrooke, Canada)
messages.body_plain:
IN PERSON COURSE ? Hierarchical modelling in ecology (HMIE01) (Universit?
de Sherbrooke, Canada)

https://www.prstats.org/course/hierarchical-modelling-in-ecology-hmie01/

Where - Longueuil Campus <https://tinyurl.com/y5pntkxc> of Universit? de
Sherbrooke

When - Monday 11th - Frtiday 15th November

Please feel free to share!


COURSE OVERVIEW - More than ever in biology and ecology we need statistical
modelling tools that go beyond the basic linear or generalized linear
models. The next evolution in statistical modelling are hierarchical
models, also known as mixed models. Hierarchical models are complex tools
that have the potential to bring your research to the next level. However,
to understand hierarchical models properly, in this course we will briefly
discuss probability theory, the frequentists and Bayesian paradigms and
(generalized) linear models before studying hierarchical models. The
discussions on hierarchical models will first focus on simple hierarchical
models and as we advance in the course, we will study the basis of
constrained hierarchical models (i.e. in space or time) and multivariate
hierarchical models. The course will be a mixed of theory and practice. To
implement the models we will discuss in the course, we will be using the
Stan programming language, a flexible programming infrastructure designed
to construct any hierarchical models from the simplest to the most complex
ones. Stan is a programming language that is typically used through a
higher-level programming language such as R, python or Julia. In this
respect, although all our examples and practical exercises will be carried
out using R, any language that can interface with Stan can be used.

By the end of the course, participants should have:

   - Learned statistical theory to better construct, apply and interpret
   different statistical models applied to biology and ecology.
   - Become familiar with primary research in statistical modelling in
   biology and ecology.
   - Gained experience in working collaboratively on issues related to the
   development and application of statistical methods.

Please email oliverhooker at prstatistics.com with any questions.

-- 
Best wishes,

Oliver

Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online course on Generalised Additive Models in R
messages.subject_clean: Online course on Generalised Additive Models in R
messages.body_plain:
Hi all,
We are excited to announce our upcoming course on Generalised Additive Models (GAMs) in R, running online from 20-24 January 2025. This course is designed to help students and researchers unlock the power of GAMs for data analysis.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/gams-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/gams-in-r/ ) 
 
What You?ll Learn:
How to fit GAMs in R using the mgcv package
Visualising GAMs and checking model assumptions
Understanding the role of splines in GAMs
Hierarchical GAMs and advanced modelling techniques
The course includes hands-on practical exercises, lectures, and group discussions, making it a comprehensive experience for those with basic RStudio knowledge.
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Course on Spatial Statistics with R - with Prof Edzer Pebesma
messages.subject_clean: Course on Spatial Statistics with R - with Prof Edzer Pebesma
messages.body_plain:
Hi  all,
there are only 5 seats left for our upcoming online course, Spatial Statistics with R (second edition), from 11-15 November. This course will cover spatial statistical methods including point pattern analysis, geostatistics, mixed models, and machine learning for spatial data. You'll also learn how to handle large datasets and satellite imagery.
 
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/spatial-statistics/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/spatial-statistics/ )
 
 
Who Should Attend:
Ideal for research students and early career researchers in biology, ecology, or related fields with an interest in spatial data analysis. Some familiarity with R and basic statistics is recommended.
Don?t miss this opportunity to enhance your skills!
 
 



Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Last Chance: Only 1 Seat Left for GLMM Course!
messages.subject_clean: Last Chance: Only 1 Seat Left for GLMM Course!
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 
Don't miss out on our upcoming course, Generalised Linear Mixed Models in R, from October 14-18! This online course will cover the essential framework for statistical analysis, focusing on how to specify, interpret, and validate GLMMs with R.
 
Only 1 seat is left! This course is ideal for graduate students and researchers familiar with generalised linear regression models who wish to deepen their understanding by incorporating random effects and correlation structures.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/glmms-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/glmms-in-r/ )
 
 
Best regards,
Carlo
